1	name: Noosphere — process Codex uploads
2	
3	# Automatically runs the Noosphere `ingest-from-codex` pipeline against
4	# queued uploads in the shared Supabase database. Three ways to fire:
5	#
6	#   1. `repository_dispatch` with event_type="process-upload" — the
7	#      primary path. The Codex `/api/upload` route triggers this
8	#      immediately after creating the Upload row, so processing
9	#      starts within seconds.
10	#
11	#   2. `schedule` (every 10 minutes) — safety net. If the immediate
12	#      webhook fires from a Vercel edge worker and the HTTPS call
13	#      fails silently (rate-limited, token revoked, partial outage),
14	#      this cron pass picks up anything still in `queued_offline`.
15	#
16	#   3. `workflow_dispatch` — manual trigger. Useful when developing
17	#      or when you upload from a CLI that didn't fire the webhook.
18	#
19	# Secrets required on the repo (Settings → Secrets → Actions):
20	#
21	#   CODEX_DATABASE_URL  — Supabase Postgres URL for CI. Prefer the
22	#                         transaction-pooler URL on port 6543 for GitHub
23	#                         Actions unless the project has reliable IPv6
24	#                         direct-host access. Do not use the session-pooler
25	#                         URL on port 5432 here; long ingest runs can exhaust
26	#                         its small session cap before the queue drains.
27	#
28	#   OPENAI_API_KEY      — optional but strongly recommended. Without
29	#                         it, the workflow runs in "naive" mode:
30	#                         regex-based claim splitting and heuristic
31	#                         contradiction detection. With it, the LLM
32	#                         extracts claims, detects contradictions
33	#                         (within + across uploads), suggests open
34	#                         questions, and proposes research follow-ups.
35	#
36	#   ANTHROPIC_API_KEY   — required for post-upload Currents opinions and
37	#                         public article generation. Ingest still works
38	#                         without it, but the public-facing commentary
39	#                         layer will abstain or fail closed.
40	#
41	#   X_BEARER_TOKEN      — required for GitHub-hosted Currents discovery.
42	#                         The always-on Currents backend may have its own
43	#                         token, but this workflow needs a repo secret if
44	#                         post-upload cloud refresh is expected to discover
45	#                         X posts itself.
46	#
47	#   CURRENTS_INGEST_ORG_ID — optional. If omitted, the workflow derives
48	#                         the public organization from the Codex DB.
49	#
50	# Exit behavior: the workflow never hard-fails on a single bad upload
51	# — individual failures are logged and the rest still process. Only
52	# a Python import / dependency crash trips the red X.
53	
54	on:
55	  repository_dispatch:
56	    types: [process-upload]
57	  schedule:
58	    # every 10 minutes — 6 passes per hour is plenty for the safety net
59	    # and well under GitHub's free tier (Actions minutes rarely hit cap
60	    # for lightweight jobs, and this one is <30s when nothing's queued).
61	    - cron: '*/10 * * * *'
62	  workflow_dispatch:
63	    inputs:
64	      upload_id:
65	        description: 'Upload ID to process (leave blank = process all queued)'
66	        required: false
67	        type: string
68	      with_llm:
69	        description: 'Use LLM mode (extracts richer claims + contradictions + questions)'
70	        required: false
71	        type: boolean
72	        default: true
73	
74	# Cancel in-flight runs of the same dispatch event so we don't double-
75	# process an upload if the webhook fires twice in quick succession.
76	# Scheduled runs each get their own concurrency group so they don't
77	# cancel in-flight manual runs.
78	concurrency:
79	  group: noosphere-process-${{ github.event.client_payload.upload_id || inputs.upload_id || github.event_name }}
80	  cancel-in-progress: false
81	
82	jobs:
83	  process:
84	    name: Run ingest-from-codex
85	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
86	    # Long-form founder recordings can be hundreds of MB and must be
87	    # chunked/transcribed in CI. Keep the workflow bounded, but do not
88	    # kill legitimate audio backfills halfway through.
89	    timeout-minutes: 120
90	    # Don't fail the whole scheduled sweep if Noosphere's
91	    # pyproject constraints shift slightly — we retry on the next
92	    # pass. Manual runs and dispatch-driven runs still surface
93	    # errors loudly because they're user-visible.
94	    continue-on-error: ${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}
95	
96	    steps:
97	      - name: Checkout
98	        uses: actions/checkout@v4
99	
100	      - name: Set up Python 3.11
101	        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
102	        with:
103	          python-version: '3.11'
104	          cache: 'pip'
105	          cache-dependency-path: noosphere/requirements.txt
106	
107	      - name: Ensure ffmpeg is available
108	        # The audio extractor's chunked path (for files > 25 MB / 25 min,
109	        # i.e. anything Whisper can't take in one shot) shells out to
110	        # ffmpeg to split into ~10-min MP3 segments. ubuntu-latest ships
111	        # with ffmpeg today, but pinning it here removes a class of
112	        # silent regressions if the runner image trims it later.
113	        run: |
114	          if ! command -v ffmpeg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
115	            sudo apt-get update -qq
116	            sudo apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends ffmpeg
117	          fi
118	          ffmpeg -version | head -n 1
119	
120	      - name: Install Noosphere
121	        working-directory: noosphere
122	        run: |
123	          python -m pip install -U pip
124	          # Install the Python requirements that the ingest pipeline
125	          # actually needs. This is a heavy install (torch +
126	          # sentence-transformers pull ~1.5GB into the pip cache),
127	          # but `actions/setup-python` caches the result keyed on
128	          # requirements.txt, so only the first run per commit pays.
129	          pip install -r requirements.txt
130	          pip install psycopg2-binary
131	          # `mutagen` powers the audio probe (size + duration) used to
132	          # decide whether to chunk before calling Whisper. It lives in
133	          # the `noosphere[audio]` optional extra alongside faster-whisper;
134	          # we only need mutagen here since the OpenAI fallback handles
135	          # transcription, so install it directly to avoid pulling in the
136	          # ~150 MB faster-whisper / ctranslate2 stack we never use on CI.
137	          pip install 'mutagen >= 1.47'
138	
139	      - name: Determine processing plan
140	        id: plan
141	        env:
142	          EVENT_NAME: ${{ github.event_name }}
143	          DISPATCH_UPLOAD_ID: ${{ github.event.client_payload.upload_id }}
144	          MANUAL_UPLOAD_ID: ${{ inputs.upload_id }}
145	        run: |
146	          # The plan's single output is `mode` and, if single-shot,
147	          # `upload_id`. A `mode=all` triggers the sweep; `mode=single`
148	          # processes one specific upload.
149	          if [ "$EVENT_NAME" = "repository_dispatch" ] && [ -n "$DISPATCH_UPLOAD_ID" ]; then
150	            echo "mode=single" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
151	            echo "upload_id=$DISPATCH_UPLOAD_ID" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
152	            echo "::notice::Processing single upload $DISPATCH_UPLOAD_ID (dispatched)"
153	          elif [ "$EVENT_NAME" = "workflow_dispatch" ] && [ -n "$MANUAL_UPLOAD_ID" ]; then
154	            echo "mode=single" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
155	            echo "upload_id=$MANUAL_UPLOAD_ID" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
156	            echo "::notice::Processing single upload $MANUAL_UPLOAD_ID (manual)"
157	          else
158	            echo "mode=all" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
159	            echo "::notice::Processing all queued uploads (sweep)"
160	          fi
161	
162	      - name: Check DB secret presence
163	        id: secretcheck
164	        env:
165	          CODEX_DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
166	          EVENT_NAME: ${{ github.event_name }}
167	        run: |
168	          # Previously: hard-failed with exit 1 when the secret wasn't
169	          # set, which turned every scheduled run into a red X even
170	          # though nothing was actually broken — it was just an unset
171	          # secret. Now: emit an actionable ::warning:: and skip the
172	          # rest of the job cleanly. The workflow run shows as green
173	          # with a visible notice so the user can see both that the
174	          # cron is alive AND that they still need to set the secret.
175	          if [ -z "${CODEX_DATABASE_URL:-}" ]; then
176	            echo "::warning::CODEX_DATABASE_URL secret is not set — skipping this run."
177	            {
178	              echo "### Setup incomplete"
179	              echo ""
180	              echo "\`CODEX_DATABASE_URL\` is not set on this repo. No queued"
181	              echo "uploads will be processed until it is."
182	              echo ""
183	              echo "**Fix:** Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions →"
184	              echo "New repository secret. Use the Supabase transaction"
185	              echo "pooler URL (port 6543) unless your direct host is known"
186	              echo "to work from GitHub Actions:"
187	              echo ""
188	              echo "    postgresql://postgres.<ref>:<pw>@aws-<n>-<region>.pooler.supabase.com:6543/postgres"
189	              echo ""
190	              echo "For best results also set \`OPENAI_API_KEY\` so the pipeline"
191	              echo "runs in LLM mode (richer claim extraction + contradictions)."
192	            } >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
193	            echo "skip=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
194	            exit 0
195	          fi
196	          echo "CODEX_DATABASE_URL is set (host hidden)"
197	          echo "skip=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
198	
199	      - name: Compute with-llm flag
200	        id: llm
201	        env:
202	          HAS_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY != '' }}
203	          WITH_LLM: ${{ inputs.with_llm }}
204	          EVENT_NAME: ${{ github.event_name }}
205	        run: |
206	          # Default: use LLM if the key is present. `workflow_dispatch`
207	          # respects the checkbox; other triggers assume "yes if key".
208	          use_llm="false"
209	          if [ "$HAS_KEY" = "true" ]; then
210	            if [ "$EVENT_NAME" = "workflow_dispatch" ]; then
211	              use_llm="$WITH_LLM"
212	            else
213	              use_llm="true"
214	            fi
215	          fi
216	          echo "use_llm=$use_llm" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
217	          if [ "$use_llm" = "true" ]; then
218	            echo "::notice::LLM mode enabled"
219	          else
220	            echo "::notice::Naive (regex) mode — set OPENAI_API_KEY secret for richer analysis"
221	          fi
222	
223	      - name: Process single upload
224	        if: steps.plan.outputs.mode == 'single' && steps.secretcheck.outputs.skip != 'true'
225	        working-directory: noosphere
226	        env:
227	          PYTHONPATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/noosphere
228	          DIRECT_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
229	          DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
230	          THESEUS_CODEX_DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
231	          OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}
232	          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
233	          THESEUS_LLM_PROVIDER: openai
234	          THESEUS_LLM_MODEL: gpt-4o-mini
235	          # Required when uploads land in Supabase Storage (filePath like
236	          # storage://… or supabase://…). The extractor mints a signed
237	          # download URL; without these it hard-fails with
238	          # "extraction_failed: SUPABASE_URL and/or SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
239	          # are not set". SUPABASE_AUDIO_BUCKET defaults to "audio" in both
240	          # the Codex and Noosphere, so only override if you've renamed it.
241	          SUPABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_URL }}
242	          SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY }}
243	          SUPABASE_AUDIO_BUCKET: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_AUDIO_BUCKET }}
244	          USE_LLM: ${{ steps.llm.outputs.use_llm }}
245	          UPLOAD_ID: ${{ steps.plan.outputs.upload_id }}
246	          NOOSPHERE_DB_SPANS: "1"
247	          # CI bypass for the Codex-side auth layer the CLI added in
248	          # f410df7. The workflow already has a higher-trust credential
249	          # (CODEX_DATABASE_URL → full Postgres access), so requiring it
250	          # to also juggle a per-device API key would be ceremony with
251	          # no marginal security. require_auth() honors this flag and
252	          # short-circuits cleanly.
253	          NOOSPHERE_SKIP_AUTH: "1"
254	        run: |
255	          set -eo pipefail
256	          args=( --upload-id "$UPLOAD_ID" )
257	          if [ "$USE_LLM" = "true" ]; then
258	            args+=( --with-llm )
259	          fi
260	          echo "+ python -m noosphere ingest-from-codex ${args[*]}"
261	          python -m noosphere ingest-from-codex "${args[@]}"
262	
263	      - name: Sweep all queued uploads
264	        if: steps.plan.outputs.mode == 'all' && steps.secretcheck.outputs.skip != 'true'
265	        working-directory: noosphere
266	        env:
267	          PYTHONPATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/noosphere
268	          DIRECT_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
269	          DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
270	          THESEUS_CODEX_DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
271	          OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}
272	          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
273	          THESEUS_LLM_PROVIDER: openai
274	          THESEUS_LLM_MODEL: gpt-4o-mini
275	          SUPABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_URL }}
276	          SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY }}
277	          SUPABASE_AUDIO_BUCKET: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_AUDIO_BUCKET }}
278	          USE_LLM: ${{ steps.llm.outputs.use_llm }}
279	          NOOSPHERE_DB_SPANS: "1"
280	          # See the single-upload step above for why this is safe here.
281	          NOOSPHERE_SKIP_AUTH: "1"
282	        run: |
283	          set -eo pipefail
284	          echo "+ python -m noosphere codex-queued --limit 25"
285	          python -m noosphere codex-queued --limit 25 > /tmp/queued.json
286	          # `codex-queued` returns clean waiting rows plus a narrow repair
287	          # class: audio uploads previously marked ingested/failed despite
288	          # having no transcript text/chunks. Transient rows are only retried
289	          # after they are stale, so active long audio transcriptions are not
290	          # duplicated but crashed jobs still recover on a later cron pass.
291	          python - <<'PY' > /tmp/ids.txt
292	          import json
293	          from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
294	
295	          clean = {"pending", "queued_offline", "", "ingested", "failed"}
296	          transient = {"processing", "extracting", "awaiting_ingest"}
297	          stale_before = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(minutes=90)
298	
299	          def parse_dt(value):
300	              if not value:
301	                  return None
302	              text = str(value).replace("Z", "+00:00")
303	              try:
304	                  parsed = datetime.fromisoformat(text)
305	              except ValueError:
306	                  return None
307	              if parsed.tzinfo is None:
308	                  parsed = parsed.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
309	              return parsed
310	
311	          rows = json.load(open("/tmp/queued.json")).get("rows", [])
312	          for row in rows:
313	              status = (row.get("status") or "").lower()
314	              if status in clean:
315	                  print(row["id"])
316	                  continue
317	              if status in transient:
318	                  updated_at = parse_dt(row.get("updatedAt"))
319	                  if updated_at and updated_at < stale_before:
320	                      print(row["id"])
321	          PY
322	          id_count=$(wc -l < /tmp/ids.txt | tr -d ' ')
323	          total_count=$(python -c "import json; print(json.load(open('/tmp/queued.json')).get('count',0))")
324	          echo "::notice::Codex has $total_count row(s) in queue states; sweep will process $id_count of them"
325	          if [ "$id_count" = "0" ]; then
326	            echo "nothing to do"
327	            exit 0
328	          fi
329	          processed=0
330	          failed=0
331	          while IFS= read -r uid; do
332	            if [ -z "$uid" ]; then continue; fi
333	            args=( --upload-id "$uid" )
334	            if [ "$USE_LLM" = "true" ]; then
335	              args+=( --with-llm )
336	            fi
337	            echo "── processing $uid ──"
338	            if python -m noosphere ingest-from-codex "${args[@]}"; then
339	              processed=$((processed + 1))
340	            else
341	              echo "::warning::ingest failed for $uid (continuing)"
342	              failed=$((failed + 1))
343	            fi
344	          done < /tmp/ids.txt
345	          echo "::notice::Sweep complete — processed=$processed failed=$failed"
346	
347	      - name: Backfill explorer data
348	        if: steps.secretcheck.outputs.skip != 'true'
349	        working-directory: noosphere
350	        env:
351	          PYTHONPATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/noosphere
352	          DIRECT_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
353	          DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
354	          THESEUS_CODEX_DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
355	          OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}
356	          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
357	          THESEUS_LLM_PROVIDER: openai
358	          THESEUS_LLM_MODEL: gpt-4o-mini
359	          SUPABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_URL }}
360	          SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY }}
361	          SUPABASE_AUDIO_BUCKET: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_AUDIO_BUCKET }}
362	          NOOSPHERE_DB_SPANS: "1"
363	          NOOSPHERE_SKIP_AUTH: "1"
364	        run: |
365	          set -eo pipefail
366	          echo "+ python -m noosphere codex-reanalyze --apply --limit 500 --max-embeddings 1000"
367	          python -m noosphere codex-reanalyze --apply --limit 500 --max-embeddings 1000
368	
369	      - name: Refresh public Currents and articles
370	        if: steps.secretcheck.outputs.skip != 'true'
371	        working-directory: noosphere
372	        env:
373	          PYTHONPATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/noosphere
374	          DIRECT_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
375	          DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
376	          THESEUS_CODEX_DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
377	          OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}
378	          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
379	          X_BEARER_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.X_BEARER_TOKEN }}
380	          CURRENTS_INGEST_ORG_ID: ${{ secrets.CURRENTS_INGEST_ORG_ID }}
381	          CURRENTS_X_SEARCH_QUERIES: ${{ secrets.CURRENTS_X_SEARCH_QUERIES }}
382	          CURRENTS_X_DISCOVERY_ENABLED: ${{ vars.CURRENTS_X_DISCOVERY_ENABLED || 'true' }}
383	          CURRENTS_X_DISCOVERY_MAX_CANDIDATES: ${{ vars.CURRENTS_X_DISCOVERY_MAX_CANDIDATES || '100' }}
384	          CURRENTS_MIN_SIGNIFICANCE_SCORE: ${{ vars.CURRENTS_MIN_SIGNIFICANCE_SCORE || '1.35' }}
385	          NOOSPHERE_ARTICLES_WEEKLY_CAP: ${{ vars.NOOSPHERE_ARTICLES_WEEKLY_CAP || '1' }}
386	          NOOSPHERE_DB_SPANS: "1"
387	          NOOSPHERE_SKIP_AUTH: "1"
388	        run: |
389	          set -eo pipefail
390	          if [ -z "${CURRENTS_INGEST_ORG_ID:-}" ]; then
391	            CURRENTS_INGEST_ORG_ID="$(python - <<'PY'
392	          import os
393	          import psycopg2
394	          from noosphere.codex_bridge import _psycopg2_compatible_url
395	
396	          url = _psycopg2_compatible_url(os.environ["DATABASE_URL"])
397	          with psycopg2.connect(url) as conn, conn.cursor() as cur:
398	              cur.execute(
399	                  'SELECT id FROM "Organization" ORDER BY CASE WHEN slug = %s THEN 0 ELSE 1 END, id ASC LIMIT 1',
400	                  ("theseus",),
401	              )
402	              row = cur.fetchone()
403	          print(row[0] if row else "")
404	          PY
405	            )"
406	            export CURRENTS_INGEST_ORG_ID
407	          fi
408	          if [ -z "${CURRENTS_INGEST_ORG_ID:-}" ]; then
409	            echo "::warning::Could not determine CURRENTS_INGEST_ORG_ID; skipping Currents/article refresh."
410	            exit 0
411	          fi
412	          if [ -z "${X_BEARER_TOKEN:-}" ]; then
413	            echo "::warning::X_BEARER_TOKEN secret is missing; GitHub cloud refresh cannot discover fresh X posts."
414	          fi
415	          if [ -z "${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY:-}" ]; then
416	            echo "::warning::ANTHROPIC_API_KEY secret is missing; Currents opinions and articles cannot be generated in GitHub."
417	          fi
418	          echo "+ python -m noosphere.currents once"
419	          python -m noosphere.currents once
420	
421	      - name: Materialize Ops rollups
422	        if: steps.secretcheck.outputs.skip != 'true'
423	        working-directory: noosphere
424	        env:
425	          PYTHONPATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/noosphere
426	          DIRECT_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
427	          DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
428	          THESEUS_CODEX_DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
429	          NOOSPHERE_SKIP_AUTH: "1"
430	        run: |
431	          set -eo pipefail
432	          echo "+ python -m noosphere ops-rollup --window-hours 24"
433	          python -m noosphere ops-rollup --window-hours 24
434

file.content:
name: Noosphere — process Codex uploads

# Automatically runs the Noosphere `ingest-from-codex` pipeline against
# queued uploads in the shared Supabase database. Three ways to fire:
#
#   1. `repository_dispatch` with event_type="process-upload" — the
#      primary path. The Codex `/api/upload` route triggers this
#      immediately after creating the Upload row, so processing
#      starts within seconds.
#
#   2. `schedule` (every 10 minutes) — safety net. If the immediate
#      webhook fires from a Vercel edge worker and the HTTPS call
#      fails silently (rate-limited, token revoked, partial outage),
#      this cron pass picks up anything still in `queued_offline`.
#
#   3. `workflow_dispatch` — manual trigger. Useful when developing
#      or when you upload from a CLI that didn't fire the webhook.
#
# Secrets required on the repo (Settings → Secrets → Actions):
#
#   CODEX_DATABASE_URL  — Supabase Postgres URL for CI. Prefer the
#                         transaction-pooler URL on port 6543 for GitHub
#                         Actions unless the project has reliable IPv6
#                         direct-host access. Do not use the session-pooler
#                         URL on port 5432 here; long ingest runs can exhaust
#                         its small session cap before the queue drains.
#
#   OPENAI_API_KEY      — optional but strongly recommended. Without
#                         it, the workflow runs in "naive" mode:
#                         regex-based claim splitting and heuristic
#                         contradiction detection. With it, the LLM
#                         extracts claims, detects contradictions
#                         (within + across uploads), suggests open
#                         questions, and proposes research follow-ups.
#
#   ANTHROPIC_API_KEY   — required for post-upload Currents opinions and
#                         public article generation. Ingest still works
#                         without it, but the public-facing commentary
#                         layer will abstain or fail closed.
#
#   X_BEARER_TOKEN      — required for GitHub-hosted Currents discovery.
#                         The always-on Currents backend may have its own
#                         token, but this workflow needs a repo secret if
#                         post-upload cloud refresh is expected to discover
#                         X posts itself.
#
#   CURRENTS_INGEST_ORG_ID — optional. If omitted, the workflow derives
#                         the public organization from the Codex DB.
#
# Exit behavior: the workflow never hard-fails on a single bad upload
# — individual failures are logged and the rest still process. Only
# a Python import / dependency crash trips the red X.

on:
  repository_dispatch:
    types: [process-upload]
  schedule:
    # every 10 minutes — 6 passes per hour is plenty for the safety net
    # and well under GitHub's free tier (Actions minutes rarely hit cap
    # for lightweight jobs, and this one is <30s when nothing's queued).
    - cron: '*/10 * * * *'
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      upload_id:
        description: 'Upload ID to process (leave blank = process all queued)'
        required: false
        type: string
      with_llm:
        description: 'Use LLM mode (extracts richer claims + contradictions + questions)'
        required: false
        type: boolean
        default: true

# Cancel in-flight runs of the same dispatch event so we don't double-
# process an upload if the webhook fires twice in quick succession.
# Scheduled runs each get their own concurrency group so they don't
# cancel in-flight manual runs.
concurrency:
  group: noosphere-process-${{ github.event.client_payload.upload_id || inputs.upload_id || github.event_name }}
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  process:
    name: Run ingest-from-codex
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    # Long-form founder recordings can be hundreds of MB and must be
    # chunked/transcribed in CI. Keep the workflow bounded, but do not
    # kill legitimate audio backfills halfway through.
    timeout-minutes: 120
    # Don't fail the whole scheduled sweep if Noosphere's
    # pyproject constraints shift slightly — we retry on the next
    # pass. Manual runs and dispatch-driven runs still surface
    # errors loudly because they're user-visible.
    continue-on-error: ${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Python 3.11
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.11'
          cache: 'pip'
          cache-dependency-path: noosphere/requirements.txt

      - name: Ensure ffmpeg is available
        # The audio extractor's chunked path (for files > 25 MB / 25 min,
        # i.e. anything Whisper can't take in one shot) shells out to
        # ffmpeg to split into ~10-min MP3 segments. ubuntu-latest ships
        # with ffmpeg today, but pinning it here removes a class of
        # silent regressions if the runner image trims it later.
        run: |
          if ! command -v ffmpeg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            sudo apt-get update -qq
            sudo apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends ffmpeg
          fi
          ffmpeg -version | head -n 1

      - name: Install Noosphere
        working-directory: noosphere
        run: |
          python -m pip install -U pip
          # Install the Python requirements that the ingest pipeline
          # actually needs. This is a heavy install (torch +
          # sentence-transformers pull ~1.5GB into the pip cache),
          # but `actions/setup-python` caches the result keyed on
          # requirements.txt, so only the first run per commit pays.
          pip install -r requirements.txt
          pip install psycopg2-binary
          # `mutagen` powers the audio probe (size + duration) used to
          # decide whether to chunk before calling Whisper. It lives in
          # the `noosphere[audio]` optional extra alongside faster-whisper;
          # we only need mutagen here since the OpenAI fallback handles
          # transcription, so install it directly to avoid pulling in the
          # ~150 MB faster-whisper / ctranslate2 stack we never use on CI.
          pip install 'mutagen >= 1.47'

      - name: Determine processing plan
        id: plan
        env:
          EVENT_NAME: ${{ github.event_name }}
          DISPATCH_UPLOAD_ID: ${{ github.event.client_payload.upload_id }}
          MANUAL_UPLOAD_ID: ${{ inputs.upload_id }}
        run: |
          # The plan's single output is `mode` and, if single-shot,
          # `upload_id`. A `mode=all` triggers the sweep; `mode=single`
          # processes one specific upload.
          if [ "$EVENT_NAME" = "repository_dispatch" ] && [ -n "$DISPATCH_UPLOAD_ID" ]; then
            echo "mode=single" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "upload_id=$DISPATCH_UPLOAD_ID" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "::notice::Processing single upload $DISPATCH_UPLOAD_ID (dispatched)"
          elif [ "$EVENT_NAME" = "workflow_dispatch" ] && [ -n "$MANUAL_UPLOAD_ID" ]; then
            echo "mode=single" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "upload_id=$MANUAL_UPLOAD_ID" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "::notice::Processing single upload $MANUAL_UPLOAD_ID (manual)"
          else
            echo "mode=all" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "::notice::Processing all queued uploads (sweep)"
          fi

      - name: Check DB secret presence
        id: secretcheck
        env:
          CODEX_DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
          EVENT_NAME: ${{ github.event_name }}
        run: |
          # Previously: hard-failed with exit 1 when the secret wasn't
          # set, which turned every scheduled run into a red X even
          # though nothing was actually broken — it was just an unset
          # secret. Now: emit an actionable ::warning:: and skip the
          # rest of the job cleanly. The workflow run shows as green
          # with a visible notice so the user can see both that the
          # cron is alive AND that they still need to set the secret.
          if [ -z "${CODEX_DATABASE_URL:-}" ]; then
            echo "::warning::CODEX_DATABASE_URL secret is not set — skipping this run."
            {
              echo "### Setup incomplete"
              echo ""
              echo "\`CODEX_DATABASE_URL\` is not set on this repo. No queued"
              echo "uploads will be processed until it is."
              echo ""
              echo "**Fix:** Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions →"
              echo "New repository secret. Use the Supabase transaction"
              echo "pooler URL (port 6543) unless your direct host is known"
              echo "to work from GitHub Actions:"
              echo ""
              echo "    postgresql://postgres.<ref>:<pw>@aws-<n>-<region>.pooler.supabase.com:6543/postgres"
              echo ""
              echo "For best results also set \`OPENAI_API_KEY\` so the pipeline"
              echo "runs in LLM mode (richer claim extraction + contradictions)."
            } >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
            echo "skip=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            exit 0
          fi
          echo "CODEX_DATABASE_URL is set (host hidden)"
          echo "skip=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Compute with-llm flag
        id: llm
        env:
          HAS_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY != '' }}
          WITH_LLM: ${{ inputs.with_llm }}
          EVENT_NAME: ${{ github.event_name }}
        run: |
          # Default: use LLM if the key is present. `workflow_dispatch`
          # respects the checkbox; other triggers assume "yes if key".
          use_llm="false"
          if [ "$HAS_KEY" = "true" ]; then
            if [ "$EVENT_NAME" = "workflow_dispatch" ]; then
              use_llm="$WITH_LLM"
            else
              use_llm="true"
            fi
          fi
          echo "use_llm=$use_llm" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          if [ "$use_llm" = "true" ]; then
            echo "::notice::LLM mode enabled"
          else
            echo "::notice::Naive (regex) mode — set OPENAI_API_KEY secret for richer analysis"
          fi

      - name: Process single upload
        if: steps.plan.outputs.mode == 'single' && steps.secretcheck.outputs.skip != 'true'
        working-directory: noosphere
        env:
          PYTHONPATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/noosphere
          DIRECT_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
          DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
          THESEUS_CODEX_DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
          OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
          THESEUS_LLM_PROVIDER: openai
          THESEUS_LLM_MODEL: gpt-4o-mini
          # Required when uploads land in Supabase Storage (filePath like
          # storage://… or supabase://…). The extractor mints a signed
          # download URL; without these it hard-fails with
          # "extraction_failed: SUPABASE_URL and/or SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
          # are not set". SUPABASE_AUDIO_BUCKET defaults to "audio" in both
          # the Codex and Noosphere, so only override if you've renamed it.
          SUPABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_URL }}
          SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY }}
          SUPABASE_AUDIO_BUCKET: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_AUDIO_BUCKET }}
          USE_LLM: ${{ steps.llm.outputs.use_llm }}
          UPLOAD_ID: ${{ steps.plan.outputs.upload_id }}
          NOOSPHERE_DB_SPANS: "1"
          # CI bypass for the Codex-side auth layer the CLI added in
          # f410df7. The workflow already has a higher-trust credential
          # (CODEX_DATABASE_URL → full Postgres access), so requiring it
          # to also juggle a per-device API key would be ceremony with
          # no marginal security. require_auth() honors this flag and
          # short-circuits cleanly.
          NOOSPHERE_SKIP_AUTH: "1"
        run: |
          set -eo pipefail
          args=( --upload-id "$UPLOAD_ID" )
          if [ "$USE_LLM" = "true" ]; then
            args+=( --with-llm )
          fi
          echo "+ python -m noosphere ingest-from-codex ${args[*]}"
          python -m noosphere ingest-from-codex "${args[@]}"

      - name: Sweep all queued uploads
        if: steps.plan.outputs.mode == 'all' && steps.secretcheck.outputs.skip != 'true'
        working-directory: noosphere
        env:
          PYTHONPATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/noosphere
          DIRECT_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
          DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
          THESEUS_CODEX_DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
          OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
          THESEUS_LLM_PROVIDER: openai
          THESEUS_LLM_MODEL: gpt-4o-mini
          SUPABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_URL }}
          SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY }}
          SUPABASE_AUDIO_BUCKET: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_AUDIO_BUCKET }}
          USE_LLM: ${{ steps.llm.outputs.use_llm }}
          NOOSPHERE_DB_SPANS: "1"
          # See the single-upload step above for why this is safe here.
          NOOSPHERE_SKIP_AUTH: "1"
        run: |
          set -eo pipefail
          echo "+ python -m noosphere codex-queued --limit 25"
          python -m noosphere codex-queued --limit 25 > /tmp/queued.json
          # `codex-queued` returns clean waiting rows plus a narrow repair
          # class: audio uploads previously marked ingested/failed despite
          # having no transcript text/chunks. Transient rows are only retried
          # after they are stale, so active long audio transcriptions are not
          # duplicated but crashed jobs still recover on a later cron pass.
          python - <<'PY' > /tmp/ids.txt
          import json
          from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone

          clean = {"pending", "queued_offline", "", "ingested", "failed"}
          transient = {"processing", "extracting", "awaiting_ingest"}
          stale_before = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(minutes=90)

          def parse_dt(value):
              if not value:
                  return None
              text = str(value).replace("Z", "+00:00")
              try:
                  parsed = datetime.fromisoformat(text)
              except ValueError:
                  return None
              if parsed.tzinfo is None:
                  parsed = parsed.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
              return parsed

          rows = json.load(open("/tmp/queued.json")).get("rows", [])
          for row in rows:
              status = (row.get("status") or "").lower()
              if status in clean:
                  print(row["id"])
                  continue
              if status in transient:
                  updated_at = parse_dt(row.get("updatedAt"))
                  if updated_at and updated_at < stale_before:
                      print(row["id"])
          PY
          id_count=$(wc -l < /tmp/ids.txt | tr -d ' ')
          total_count=$(python -c "import json; print(json.load(open('/tmp/queued.json')).get('count',0))")
          echo "::notice::Codex has $total_count row(s) in queue states; sweep will process $id_count of them"
          if [ "$id_count" = "0" ]; then
            echo "nothing to do"
            exit 0
          fi
          processed=0
          failed=0
          while IFS= read -r uid; do
            if [ -z "$uid" ]; then continue; fi
            args=( --upload-id "$uid" )
            if [ "$USE_LLM" = "true" ]; then
              args+=( --with-llm )
            fi
            echo "── processing $uid ──"
            if python -m noosphere ingest-from-codex "${args[@]}"; then
              processed=$((processed + 1))
            else
              echo "::warning::ingest failed for $uid (continuing)"
              failed=$((failed + 1))
            fi
          done < /tmp/ids.txt
          echo "::notice::Sweep complete — processed=$processed failed=$failed"

      - name: Backfill explorer data
        if: steps.secretcheck.outputs.skip != 'true'
        working-directory: noosphere
        env:
          PYTHONPATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/noosphere
          DIRECT_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
          DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
          THESEUS_CODEX_DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
          OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
          THESEUS_LLM_PROVIDER: openai
          THESEUS_LLM_MODEL: gpt-4o-mini
          SUPABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_URL }}
          SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY }}
          SUPABASE_AUDIO_BUCKET: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_AUDIO_BUCKET }}
          NOOSPHERE_DB_SPANS: "1"
          NOOSPHERE_SKIP_AUTH: "1"
        run: |
          set -eo pipefail
          echo "+ python -m noosphere codex-reanalyze --apply --limit 500 --max-embeddings 1000"
          python -m noosphere codex-reanalyze --apply --limit 500 --max-embeddings 1000

      - name: Refresh public Currents and articles
        if: steps.secretcheck.outputs.skip != 'true'
        working-directory: noosphere
        env:
          PYTHONPATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/noosphere
          DIRECT_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
          DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
          THESEUS_CODEX_DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
          OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
          X_BEARER_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.X_BEARER_TOKEN }}
          CURRENTS_INGEST_ORG_ID: ${{ secrets.CURRENTS_INGEST_ORG_ID }}
          CURRENTS_X_SEARCH_QUERIES: ${{ secrets.CURRENTS_X_SEARCH_QUERIES }}
          CURRENTS_X_DISCOVERY_ENABLED: ${{ vars.CURRENTS_X_DISCOVERY_ENABLED || 'true' }}
          CURRENTS_X_DISCOVERY_MAX_CANDIDATES: ${{ vars.CURRENTS_X_DISCOVERY_MAX_CANDIDATES || '100' }}
          CURRENTS_MIN_SIGNIFICANCE_SCORE: ${{ vars.CURRENTS_MIN_SIGNIFICANCE_SCORE || '1.35' }}
          NOOSPHERE_ARTICLES_WEEKLY_CAP: ${{ vars.NOOSPHERE_ARTICLES_WEEKLY_CAP || '1' }}
          NOOSPHERE_DB_SPANS: "1"
          NOOSPHERE_SKIP_AUTH: "1"
        run: |
          set -eo pipefail
          if [ -z "${CURRENTS_INGEST_ORG_ID:-}" ]; then
            CURRENTS_INGEST_ORG_ID="$(python - <<'PY'
          import os
          import psycopg2
          from noosphere.codex_bridge import _psycopg2_compatible_url

          url = _psycopg2_compatible_url(os.environ["DATABASE_URL"])
          with psycopg2.connect(url) as conn, conn.cursor() as cur:
              cur.execute(
                  'SELECT id FROM "Organization" ORDER BY CASE WHEN slug = %s THEN 0 ELSE 1 END, id ASC LIMIT 1',
                  ("theseus",),
              )
              row = cur.fetchone()
          print(row[0] if row else "")
          PY
            )"
            export CURRENTS_INGEST_ORG_ID
          fi
          if [ -z "${CURRENTS_INGEST_ORG_ID:-}" ]; then
            echo "::warning::Could not determine CURRENTS_INGEST_ORG_ID; skipping Currents/article refresh."
            exit 0
          fi
          if [ -z "${X_BEARER_TOKEN:-}" ]; then
            echo "::warning::X_BEARER_TOKEN secret is missing; GitHub cloud refresh cannot discover fresh X posts."
          fi
          if [ -z "${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY:-}" ]; then
            echo "::warning::ANTHROPIC_API_KEY secret is missing; Currents opinions and articles cannot be generated in GitHub."
          fi
          echo "+ python -m noosphere.currents once"
          python -m noosphere.currents once

      - name: Materialize Ops rollups
        if: steps.secretcheck.outputs.skip != 'true'
        working-directory: noosphere
        env:
          PYTHONPATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/noosphere
          DIRECT_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
          DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
          THESEUS_CODEX_DATABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.CODEX_DATABASE_URL }}
          NOOSPHERE_SKIP_AUTH: "1"
        run: |
          set -eo pipefail
          echo "+ python -m noosphere ops-rollup --window-hours 24"
          python -m noosphere ops-rollup --window-hours 24

content.text:
`/ops` redesigned as a triage-first health console. Sections in fixed order:

1. **Broken now** (red) — only renders cards if something is actually wrong: missing `GITHUB_DISPATCH_TOKEN`, unreachable Currents backend, `disabled_reasons` from Currents, last-cycle errors, failed uploads in last 24h (with per-row error lines), uploads stuck >90 min in transient status (same threshold the GH Actions sweep uses), embedding backfill failure, unacknowledged alerts.
2. **Running / queued** (amber) — upload queue counts with status breakdown, in-flight observability traces, last Currents cycle stats.
3. **Healthy / recent** (green) — last successful ingest, full Currents pipeline summary, embedding coverage, workflow catalog (links out to GitHub Actions UI — the Codex can't read run status without a token, and the card says so), auto-processing env booleans (presence only, never values).
4. **Diagnostics** — link cards to the existing `?panel=` sub-pages (observability, provenance, contradictions, decay, rigor-gate, methods, eval, etc.) so nothing is hidden.

Plus a standalone **always-on worker** note flagging that Currents/articles/forecasts depend on a long-running container (`Dockerfile.scheduler`) which can't run on Vercel — color reflects whether the last cycle is recent (`success`), unknown/stale (`warning`), or backend unreachable (`danger`). Never claims "fixed."

Files:
